Next, use those coordinates to get the current weather at each of the 3 parks' locations. After that, search Wikipedia for the article 'Rocky Mountain National Park' and extract key facts about its geology (specifically look for any mention of elevation figures in feet or meters). Then use the Google Maps geocode tool to geocode the address 'Estes Park, Colorado' and record the exact latitude and longitude returned. Finally, use those geocoded coordinates to calculate the walking distance and travel time from 'Estes Park, Colorado' to 'Grand Lake, Colorado'. In your final answer, report: (1) the name and park code of each of the 3 Colorado parks you retrieved details for, along with their entrance fees, (2) the current temperature (in °F or °C as returned) at each of the 3 park locations, (3) the highest elevation figure you found in the Rocky Mountain National Park Wikipedia article, and (4) the walking distance and estimated travel time from Estes Park to Grand Lake as returned by Google Maps.
